meaning: adj.商人的,商业的,经商的,重商主义的,以金钱为目的的
examples: Its geographical location stimulated overseas mercantile enterprise.
San Francisco and New York are important mercantile ports in America.
As feudalism decayed in the West it gave rise to a mercantile class.
You would short euro futures on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange or the New York Board of Trade.
The Chicago Mercantile Exchange floated stock in 2002, and the International Securities Exchange went public earlier this year.
